content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 4,
1994
Noel T.
Charonnat
President
Donner Summit Public Utilities District Board
P.O.
Box 610
Soda Springs,
CA 95728
Re:
Your Request For Advice
Our File No.
1-94-099
Dear Mr.
Charonnat:
We write in response to your request for general advice
regarding possible conflict of interest issues under the Political
Reform Act
(the “Act”).
Since your request is one for general
information,
we treat it as
a request for informal assistance.
1
You write that one of your newly elected board members and
the general manager of the district are cohabitants.
As the
general manager serves at the discretion of the board,
you ask
what conflict of interest provisions apply to the board member in
her official capacity.
We enclose a copy of the Roff Advice Letter,
No.
A-89—106
which we hope will be of instructional benefit to you.
The advice
contained in that letter is current.
If you have any specific
questions or need additional information please call the
undersigned at
(916)
322—5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Couns
/1
By:
Counsel,
Legal Division
Enclosure
1
Informal assistance does not provide hë requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 I
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 I
(916) 322-5660

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission April 4, 1994 Noel T. Charonnat President Donner Summit Public Utilities District Board P.O. Box 610 Soda Springs, CA 95728 Re: Your Request For Advice Our File No. 1-94-099 Dear Mr. Charonnat: We write in response to your request for general advice regarding possible conflict of interest issues under the Political Reform Act (the “Act”). Since your request is one for general information, we treat it as a request for informal assistance. 1 You write that one of your newly elected board members and the general manager of the district are cohabitants. As the general manager serves at the discretion of the board, you ask what conflict of interest provisions apply to the board member in her official capacity. We enclose a copy of the Roff Advice Letter, No. A-89—106 which we hope will be of instructional benefit to you. The advice contained in that letter is current. If you have any specific questions or need additional information please call the undersigned at (916) 322—5901. Sincerely, Steven G. Churchwell General Couns /1 By: Counsel, Legal Division Enclosure 1 Informal assistance does not provide hë requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c) (3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 I Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 I (916) 322-5660
